questions.q: How many years after the Kurukshetra war did the destruction of the Yadavas take place?
questions.explanation: Gandhari's curse ripened exactly thirty-six years after the war, as she had foretold.

questions.q: Whose curse foretold that the Yadava race would destroy itself?
questions.explanation: Standing among her dead sons after the war, Gandhari cursed Krishna that his kinsmen would slay one another in thirty-six years.

questions.q: What cursed object became the instrument of the Yadavas' destruction?
questions.explanation: The sages' curse produced an iron pestle (musala), which gives the Mausala Parva its name.

questions.q: How did Krishna meet his death?
options: He drowned when the sea took Dwarka
He was shot in the heel by a hunter's arrow
He was slain by an iron mace in the brawl
He walked into a forest fire
questions.explanation: The hunter Jara's arrow pierced Krishna's heel, the one vulnerable spot, as he lay in yoga beneath a tree.

questions.q: What was the name of the hunter whose arrow killed Krishna?
questions.explanation: The hunter Jara shot Krishna by mistake and was forgiven by the dying avatar.

questions.q: What happened to the city of Dwarka seven days after Krishna's death?
options: It was sacked by Abhira robbers
It burned in a great fire
It was swallowed by the sea
It was buried in a sandstorm
questions.explanation: Exactly as foretold, the ocean rose and submerged Dwarka on the seventh day after Krishna departed.

questions.q: Which book of the Mahabharata narrates the destruction of the Yadavas?
questions.explanation: The Mausala Parva, the book of the iron pestle, is the sixteenth of the epic's eighteen books.

questions.q: At which holy place on the seashore did the Yadavas slaughter one another?
questions.explanation: The Yadavas had gone on pilgrimage to Prabhasa on the western coast when the drunken brawl erupted.

questions.q: What grew from the powdered iron pestle that was cast into the sea?
options: Razor-sharp eraka grass on the shore
A reef of black iron rocks
questions.explanation: The powder washed ashore at Prabhasa and sprang up as eraka grass, each blade fated to become a weapon.

questions.q: Which elder brother of Krishna also gave up his life at Prabhasa?
questions.explanation: Balarama sat in meditation by the sea and left his body before Krishna's own death.

questions.q: Which son of Krishna was dressed as a pregnant woman to mock the visiting sages?
questions.explanation: The Yadava youths disguised Samba, Krishna's son by Jambavati, and asked the sages what child he would bear.

questions.q: What did the angered sages declare the disguised Samba would deliver?
options: A serpent that would poison the city
An iron pestle that would destroy the race
A stillborn child as a mark of doom
A firebrand that would burn Dwarka
questions.explanation: The sages cursed that Samba would bring forth an iron pestle that would annihilate the Vrishnis and Andhakas.

questions.q: What set off the fatal brawl among the Yadavas at Prabhasa?
options: A dispute over dividing the pilgrimage offerings
An insult flung at Balarama's plough
Drunken taunts about the night raid on the Pandava camp
A quarrel over a rigged game of dice
questions.explanation: In his cups, Satyaki taunted Kritavarma for slaughtering sleeping men in Ashwatthama's night raid, and blood answered words.

questions.q: Whom did Satyaki behead at the start of the brawl at Prabhasa?
questions.explanation: Satyaki struck off Kritavarma's head, and the enraged Bhojas and Andhakas then fell upon Satyaki and Pradyumna.

questions.q: What happened when the enraged Yadavas plucked the eraka grass by the shore?
options: It crumbled to ash in their hands
Each blade became an iron club in their grip
It cut their palms so they bled to death
It coiled around their arms like serpents
questions.explanation: Every blade of the cursed grass turned to iron in their hands, and with these bolts they slaughtered each other.

questions.q: How did Balarama leave the world at Prabhasa?
options: He walked into the sea and drowned
He was struck down by an iron mace
He burned himself on a funeral pyre
A white serpent left his mouth and entered the ocean
questions.explanation: As Balarama sat in meditation, the great serpent Shesha, his true form, issued from his mouth and was received by the ocean.

questions.q: Who carried the news of the Yadava massacre to the Pandavas in Hastinapura?
questions.explanation: At Krishna's command his charioteer Daruka rode to Hastinapura to summon Arjuna.

questions.q: Which Pandava traveled to Dwarka to evacuate the survivors of the massacre?
questions.explanation: Arjuna came to Dwarka, performed the funeral rites, and led the women and citizens away before the sea closed in.

questions.q: What humiliation befell Arjuna while escorting the survivors of Dwarka?
options: He lost the road in the desert for a month
The sea pursued and scattered the caravan
Takshaka's serpents attacked the camp at night
Abhira robbers carried off the women as his weapons failed
questions.explanation: When Abhira bandits attacked, the Gandiva grew heavy and Arjuna's astras would not come, a sign that his era had ended.

questions.q: Who was installed as ruler of the surviving Yadavas at Indraprastha?
questions.explanation: Arjuna installed Vajra, Krishna's great-grandson, as king of the remnant of the Yadavas at Indraprastha.

questions.q: What was the hunter Jara doing when he loosed the arrow that killed Krishna?
options: Stalking what he took for a deer
Defending himself from a wild boar
questions.explanation: Jara mistook Krishna's moving foot for a crouching deer and shot the fatal arrow into his heel.

questions.q: How did the Yadavas try to neutralize the cursed iron pestle after Samba delivered it?
options: They buried it beneath a temple of Shiva
They threw it whole into a mountain chasm
They ground it to powder and cast it into the sea
They melted it down and forged it into a plough
questions.explanation: The pestle was pounded to dust and thrown into the ocean, but the curse returned as razor grass on the shore.

questions.q: What became of the one lump of the iron pestle that could not be ground down?
options: It was hidden in Dwarka's treasury and lost with the city
A fish swallowed it and a hunter forged it into an arrowhead
Balarama hurled it beyond the horizon into the deep
Krishna wore it as an amulet until the day he died
questions.explanation: A fish swallowed the unbreakable fragment, and the hunter Jara found it in the fish's belly and tipped his arrow with it.

questions.q: Which sages, in the common telling of the Mausala Parva, were mocked by the Yadava youths?
questions.explanation: Vishvamitra, Kanva, and Narada, visiting Dwarka, answered the prank with the curse of the iron pestle.

questions.q: How did Krishna respond when Gandhari pronounced her curse after the war?
options: He accepted it calmly, saying the Yadavas could be destroyed only by themselves
He begged her to withdraw the words before they took root
He countered the curse with a blessing upon her line
He laughed and declared that no curse could touch an avatar
questions.explanation: Krishna accepted the curse with equanimity, knowing the invincible Yadavas could fall to no hand but their own.

questions.q: What standing prohibition did the Yadavas violate at the Prabhasa festival?
options: The vow never to draw weapons against kin
The rule against feasting before the sages had eaten
The order to remain within the walls of Dwarka
The ban on making and drinking liquor
questions.explanation: Though strong drink had been banned in Dwarka by royal decree, the Yadavas drank deep at Prabhasa and fell to quarreling.

questions.q: Which of Krishna's own sons fell in the brawl at Prabhasa?
questions.explanation: Pradyumna was slain before Krishna's eyes, along with Samba, Charudeshna, and his grandson Aniruddha.

questions.q: Under what kind of tree was Krishna resting when the hunter's arrow struck him?
options: A shami tree by an old battlefield
A pippal (fig) tree in the forest
A sandalwood tree by the sea
questions.explanation: Krishna lay in yoga beneath a pippal tree with his foot upon his thigh when Jara's arrow found his heel.

questions.q: Who ordered the iron pestle ground to powder and thrown into the sea?
questions.explanation: Ugrasena, also called Ahuka, the Yadava king, had the pestle pounded to dust and cast into the ocean to thwart the curse.

questions.q: What omen involving Krishna's own emblems preceded the destruction of the Yadavas?
options: Panchajanya cracked apart in his hands
Garuda abandoned the palace roof forever
The Sudarshana discus and his chariot rose to the sky and vanished
His banner turned black in a single night
questions.explanation: The discus given by Agni flew up to heaven and Krishna's chariot was carried away over the sea, signs that his work on earth was done.
